articles.title: Google says Polymarket bets showing up in News was an ‘error’
articles.summary: Google accidentally displayed Polymarket betting odds in its News results, prompting the company to call it an "error" and presumably remove the feature. The slip-up reveals Google's internal experiments with integrating prediction market data into search results, which could massively boost mainstream adoption if they decide to make it permanent.

articles.title: ‘Abhorrent’: the inside story of the Polymarket gamblers betting millions on war
articles.summary: Polymarket users are placing massive bets on war outcomes, sparking outrage over the ethics of profiting from human tragedy. The controversy threatens to fuel regulatory crackdowns on prediction markets, especially as platforms struggle to balance free speech with public relations disasters.

articles.title: Polymarket’s $269 Million Question: Did U.S. Forces ‘Enter’ Iran?
articles.summary: A massive $269 million Polymarket bet is riding on whether U.S. military forces have officially "entered" Iran, likely sparked by recent military tensions in the region. The semantic parsing of what constitutes "entering" a country could make or break fortunes, highlighting how prediction markets turn geopolitical crises into high-stakes grammar debates.

articles.title: Prediction markets ramp up DC messaging
articles.summary: Prediction market platforms are flooding Washington with lobbyists and policy papers as they push for clearer regulations and broader legitimacy. The timing matters because favorable rules could unlock institutional money and mainstream adoption, while hostile regulation could kneecap the industry before it hits its stride.

articles.title: Prediction markets have made betting easier than ever—and young men are paying the price
articles.summary: Young men are getting hooked on sports betting and prediction markets at unprecedented rates, racking up serious losses as mobile apps make wagering as simple as ordering food. This gambling surge threatens to poison the well for legitimate prediction markets, as regulators and lawmakers struggle to distinguish between casino-style betting apps and information aggregation platforms.

articles.title: Pile of Famous Athletes Quietly Invested in Kalshi Months Ago
articles.summary: A bunch of big-name athletes dropped money into Kalshi well before the prediction market became a household name during election season. Smart money often moves early, and when star athletes are quietly backing a platform months ahead of mainstream adoption, it signals they see serious upside in prediction markets going mainstream.

articles.title: Weather Prediction Markets Are Booming. Can They Improve Forecasts?
articles.summary: Weather prediction markets are exploding as traders bet on everything from hurricane paths to temperature records, turning meteorology into a gambling opportunity. If these markets can actually outperform traditional forecasts, they'll prove that crowdsourced betting beats expert analysis in yet another domain.

articles.title: Polymarket Orban Odds Drop After Vance Visit: $9M Wagered
articles.summary: Viktor Orban's odds of achieving some political outcome on Polymarket dropped following JD Vance's recent visit to Hungary, with $9M in total bets placed on the market. The sharp movement shows how high-profile diplomatic visits can instantly shift betting sentiment, giving traders who track political calendars and international relationships a clear edge over casual bettors.

articles.title: Exclusive | White House Warns Staff Not to Place Bets on Prediction Markets Amid Iran War
articles.summary: The White House has reportedly issued internal warnings to staff prohibiting them from betting on prediction markets related to potential military conflict with Iran. This signals that senior officials view prediction markets as legitimate information sources worth restricting, which ironically validates their growing influence in policy circles while creating potential insider trading concerns around geopolitical events.

articles.title: 'We have a gambling epidemic': WA AG explains why the state is trying to ban Kalshi and other predictive markets
articles.summary: Washington's Attorney General is pushing to ban Kalshi and other prediction markets, calling widespread betting a "gambling epidemic." This signals a broader regulatory crackdown that could force prediction markets to choose between compliance costs and market access in major states.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Turned the World Into a Casino
articles.summary: Prediction markets have exploded beyond politics and finance into everything from celebrity deaths to weather patterns, creating a global betting ecosystem where literally anything can become a tradeable outcome. This massive expansion means more liquidity and opportunities for sharp bettors, but also attracts casual gamblers who don't understand the fundamentals, potentially creating mispriced markets ripe for exploitation.

articles.title: Bank of America Sees a $1.1 Trillion Market for Sports Event Betting in US
articles.summary: Bank of America analysts project the US sports betting market could reach $1.1 trillion in total handle, signaling massive growth potential beyond current estimates. This validates what prediction market operators already know: Americans have an enormous appetite for putting money behind their opinions, whether on touchdown passes or presidential races.
